seoTitle: Air Scrubbers & AFDs for Job Site Air Quality | CARSI
metaDescription: Free CARSI course on using air scrubbers and AFDs on the job site: filters, HEPA, negative pressure, ACH calculations, maintenance and compliance.
imageAlt: Restoration technician setting up a HEPA air scrubber for negative pressure containment on an Australian job site.

og.title: Air Scrubbers & AFDs — Job-Site Air Quality | CARSI
og.description: A free CARSI course on air filtration devices — how air scrubbers work, choosing HEPA, carbon and pre-filters, recirculation vs negative pressure, calculating Air Changes per Hour and maintenance.

faq.q: What is the difference between recirculation and negative pressure mode?
faq.a: In recirculation mode the AFD filters air and returns it to the same room to reduce particulate levels. Negative pressure mode vents filtered air outside to create containment, drawing air into the work area so contaminants cannot escape — used for mould, asbestos or infection control.

faq.q: How do you calculate Air Changes per Hour (ACH)?
faq.a: ACH = (CFM × 60) ÷ room volume in cubic feet. For example, a 500 CFM scrubber in a 10,000 cubic foot room gives 3 ACH. The course notes typical targets of 2–4 ACH for general work, 4–6 for mould remediation and 6–12 for asbestos or infection control.

HEPA filters capture 99.97% of airborne particles 0.3 microns or larger, including mould spores, bacteria and fine dust. A pre-filter catches larger debris first, and an activated carbon filter can be added for odours and VOCs.

faq.q: Does an air scrubber remove the need for PPE?
faq.a: No. An AFD is one layer of protection, not a replacement for PPE. Appropriate respirators, gloves, goggles and coveralls should still be worn, especially during demolition or work involving hazardous materials.

courseJsonLd.name: Using Air Scrubbers and AFDs to Improve Job Site Air Quality
courseJsonLd.description: A free CARSI course on operating air filtration devices (air scrubbers) to control airborne contaminants on job sites, covering filters, recirculation and negative pressure, ACH calculations, maintenance, safety and documentation.
courseJsonLd.coursePrerequisites: None — suitable for restoration professionals, contractors and environmental technicians.
